Educating women, particular, has unmatched transformative power.
not only empowers them but also has positive effect their family and surroundings.
Women with more education tend have fewer children, which benefits them, their families and the society large.
Education also one the most powerful elements improving people’s health.
saves the lives millions mothers and children, helps prevent diseases, and essential component efforts reduce malnutrition.
Educated mothers are better informed about diseases; therefore, they take preventative measures, recognize signs illness early and tend use health care services more often.
create awareness regarding the importance girl child eductaion, SOC Films collaboration with Chime Change, created series short documentaries young girls who want achieve their dreams and aspirations seek excellence various professions future whether it's sports, literature, science, math, activism, and forth.
Chime for Change, global movement aimed strengthening voices speaking out support the cause promoting justice, health and education for girls and women the world over.
These documentaries were screened event New York <hide>th October this year, which also celebrated the International Day the Girl Child.
The event was also featured Huffington Post.
Areeba Fatima, student Dawood Public School, one the biggest girls’ school Karachi, also featured one the documentaries where she expresses her desire become diplomat able represent Pakistan the and paint positive picture the country.
The documentary can viewed here: http://vimeo.com/<hide> Dawood Public School among the very few schools that aim empower women giving them the confidence facing the world through participation many extracurricular activities including sports, debates, performing arts etc.

original text:
The Chi.
Lena Waithe’s critically acclaimed Showtime series returns for Season The struggles that Black men have face Chicago are the forefront this time around: Picking several months after the events Season the plight Black men the Windy City are front and center: Aspiring chef Brandon (Jason Mitchell) now runs food truck and trying break into the world culinary competitions.
Kevin (Alex Hibbert) still juggling middle school and therapy help deal with his PTSD after his involvement two shootings.
Emmett (Jacob Latimore) forced reconnect with his long lost father after his mother kicks him out the house.
Ronnie (Ntare Guam Mabaho Mwine) battles alcoholism while adjusting life prison after confessing murdering Brandon’s brother.
Though the emphasis the men the show, the ladies are not left out: Ronnie’s grandmother Miss Ethel (LaDonna Tittle), finds herself the center battle gentrify her neighborhood; Emmett’s mother Jada (Yolanda Ross) who Ethel’s home care nurse, trying build new life outside work and motherhood.
Brandon’s girlfriend Jerrika (Tiffany Boone), part real estate deal that can dramatically alter the make the South Side.
Though violence and its aftermath casts large shadow over all the characters, this series remains celebration the city and those who live it.
Rich detail and multi-dimensional characters, look for another solid season for one TV’s best shows.
The Chi airs Sunday nights <hide>pm Eastern Showtime.
You can also www.sho.com for more information.
Satan Adam: The Music Will Take You Higher.
Scott Balcerek directed this new documentary about unlikely musical partnership between Sterling ‘Satan’ Magee, southern-born guitarist and Adam Gussow, white, younger, Ivy League educated New York suburbanite harmonica player.
traces how Magee, after stints with such greats Ray Charles, James Brown, King Curtis, got tired seeing how black artists got exploited and took the streets Harlem play strictly for the people.
chance encounter with Gussow, grad school drop out and part-time street performer turned into year collaboration that took from the streets New York stages all over the world, cover story Living Blues magazine and appearance the concert film and soundtrack Rattle Hum.
The doc also covers Gussow’s efforts reconnect with Magee, who, after suffering nervous breakdown, stopped performing and all but disappeared, well their dramatic return <hide>.
Though music plays formative role the love and mutual respect they have for each other what really fuels the film.
doc that will move and inspire.
Satan Adam: The Music Will Take You Higher opens New York this weekend and select theaters starting April <hide>th.
You can also for more information.
Record Store Day.
Conceived <hide> way celebrate the communal spirit attached around seeking out and buying music your local store, Record Store Day has now become international holiday for music fans, with some the top artists the world contributing material exclusively for the day.
Pearl Jam are this year’s Ambassadors and you’ll have the chance pick super rare releases Green, Perfect Circle, Mascis, surprise appearances and much more.
There will also live events various stores across the country, making this yet again the must see event for true record heads.
Record Store Day will take place Saturday, April <hide>th.
You can for more information.
New York City: Too Fast Live, Young Die: Punk Graphics <hide>-<hide>.
The punk movement that emerged out New York and London not only produced incredible music, also unleashed wave provocative fashion and graphic art.
The Museum Arts And Design has just launched new exhibit that captures some the most memorable images that graced fliers and fanzines and ran direct counter what has being presented mainstream media.
It’s great primer for those just getting into punk culture great refresher for long time fans.
Too Fast Live, Young Die: Punk Graphics <hide>-<hide> will The Museum Arts And Design through August <hide>th.
You can also www.madmuseum.org for more information.
Los Angeles: Annie Leibovitz: The Early Years <hide>-<hide>.
This the final weekend catch Hauser Wirth’s extensive exhibit covering the legendary photographer’s formative years working Rolling Stone.
features over <hide> photos including her iconic shots John Lennon, The Rolling Stones, Patti Smith and much more.
There’s also series personal photos her family, heroes and mentors and much more.
One chance get the work all time great.
Annie Leibovitz: The Early Years <hide>-<hide> will the Hauser Wirth Gallery February <hide>th through April <hide>th.
You can also www.hauserwirth.com for more information.
The TCM Classic Film Festival.
Celebrating its <hide>th year, The TCM Classic Film Festival gathers cinema lovers from all over the world experience some the greatest films the way was meant seen: the big screen.
Butch Cassidy The Sundance Kid, The Defiant Ones, The Godfather Marty, and The Sound Music will air alongside more recent classics such The Right Thing, The Shawshank Redemption, Raiders The Lost Ark and When Harry Meet Sally.

Satan Adam: The Music Will Take You Higher.
Scott Balcerek directed this new documentary about unlikely musical partnership between Sterling ‘Satan’ Magee, southern-born guitarist and Adam Gussow, white, younger, Ivy League educated New York suburbanite harmonica player.
traces how Magee, after stints with such greats Ray Charles, James Brown, King Curtis, got tired seeing how black artists got exploited and took the streets Harlem play strictly for the people.
chance encounter with Gussow, grad school drop out and part-time street performer turned into year collaboration that took from the streets New York stages all over the world, cover story Living Blues magazine and appearance the concert film and soundtrack Rattle Hum.
The doc also covers Gussow’s efforts reconnect with Magee, who, after suffering nervous breakdown, stopped performing and all but disappeared, well their dramatic return <hide>.

doc that will move and inspire.
